revision.comment: Adding [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category|Ozan Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.sabae.fukui.jp/kanko/sightseeing/ozankohun.html Sabae city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sabae-ozan.html Fukui Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabae, Fukui]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Echizen Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōzan Kofun Cluster]][[en:Ōzan Kofun Cluster]]

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ozan Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.sabae.fukui.jp/kanko/sightseeing/ozankohun.html Sabae city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sabae-ozan.html Fukui Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

revision.comment: Bot: classify by valid enwiki interwikis
revision.text:
{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ozan Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.sabae.fukui.jp/kanko/sightseeing/ozankohun.html Sabae city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sabae-ozan.html Fukui Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ozan Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.sabae.fukui.jp/kanko/sightseeing/ozankohun.html Sabae city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sabae-ozan.html Fukui Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

All of the tombs had [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sabae, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[Sabae Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ozan Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.sabae.fukui.jp/kanko/sightseeing/ozankohun.html Sabae city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sabae-ozan.html Fukui Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], mostly [[Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[Tōkai region]] and [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Sabae Station|Sabae Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Sabae Station|Sabae Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Brial mounds in Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | native_name = 王山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ouzan2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōzan Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|56|23.0|N|136|11|5.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōzan Kofun Cluster'''|王山古墳群|Ōzan Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the Hinode area of the city of [[:en:Sabae, Fukui|Sabae, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1967.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138254 |title= 王山古墳群 おうざんこふんぐん|trans-title= Ōzan Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōzan Kofun Cluster is located on an isolated hill with an elevation of 62 meters near the centre of the modern city of Sabae, and consists of 49 tumuli dating to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[Kofun period]]. The area was originally designated a protected area in 1942; however, after a detailed survey was conducted in 1967, it was determined that the area under protection did not include all of the site, whereas conversely a large portion of the protected area was not necessary. The borders of the area were revised and the new boundaries became a National Historic Site in 1967<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A total of eleven tumuli were excavated in 1965 (No. 1–7, 9, 25, 31 and 32), of which No. 1, 3, 4, and 7 were {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square mounds|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), eight to twelve meters on each side with a height of one to two meters, and were dated to the late Yayoi period. All of the tombs had the remnants of a wooden [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]], with the exception of No. 4, which had two. All of the tombs had [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], mostly [[:en:Yayoi pottery|pottery fragments]], with the exception of No.1, which was completely bare. Tomb No. 3 had goods from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] and [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan, indicating the existence of long-distance trade.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tombs No. 5, 7, 9 and 25 were also square tombs, eight to fourteen meters on a side, but were dated from the early Kofun period, as indicated by the presence of fragments of swords and other weapons, and metal horse fittings found inside. Tomb No. 31 was a round tumulus with diameter of twenty meters and height of two meters, and Tomb No. 32 was also a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), but with diameter of eight meters. Both were also dated to the early Kofun period, and contained many artifacts. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown. It is one of many similar tumuli which have been discovered in the area, which has been densely populated since the Yayoi and Kofun periods, and from its scale it must have been the tomb of a local chieftain or other person of importance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now preserved as a public park with walking trails, and is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Sabae Station|Sabae Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
